display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
switchDelay: macOS drops
rapid back-to-back gotoSpace calls

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
syncInProgress + debounceSeconds:
prevents the watcher reacting
to its own gotoSpace

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
lastActiveSpaces snapshot:
identifies which display
the user actually switched

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Ignore window =
numTargets × switchDelay + debounceSeconds
≈ 1.4s for a 3-display group with defaults

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
User switches Space
on trigger display

span.p:
syncInProgress = false
refresh lastActiveSpaces
watcher re-enabled

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
return — drop event
⚠ NOT queued, NOT retried.
Most events here are the
Spoon's own gotoSpace echoes.
A real user switch arriving
during the ignore window is
silently lost: lastActiveSpaces
is re-snapshotted at the end,
folding it into the baseline.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

svg.text: SpacesSync — runtime sync workflow (CURRENT, as of v0.2)
